question_text: Which statement best describes why a longer crowbar is more effective at lifting a load than a shorter one?

options.label: A
options.text: It is heavier and creates more force

options.label: B
options.text: It increases the perpendicular distance from the pivot, creating a larger moment with the same applied force

options.label: C
options.text: It reduces the weight of the load being lifted

options.label: D
options.text: It moves faster and requires less time to lift the load

correct_answer: It increases the perpendicular distance from the pivot, creating a larger moment with the same applied force
explanation: A longer crowbar is more effective because it increases the perpendicular distance from the pivot point. Since moment = force × perpendicular distance, a larger distance means a larger turning effect (moment) is produced by the same applied force. This makes it easier to lift the load.
